# Tool &amp; Computational Resource Disclosure

_Generated by `tools/build_tool_disclosure.py`. This section implements the Leiden Declaration on AI and Mathematics' call for a tool and computational resource disclosure. See [LEIDEN-ALIGNMENT.md](LEIDEN-ALIGNMENT.md)._

> **Authorship & responsibility.** AI systems listed here are *tools*. Credit and responsibility for every result in this repository remain with the human maintainer; no result is attributed to an automated system. `canClaimAGI: false` everywhere.

## Trained / fine-tuned base models

- **google/gemma-3-4b-it** — method(s): lora; 1 adapter entry (from `training/adapters/registry.jsonl`)

## Tool servers (MCP, from `.mcp.json`)

- **runpod** — `npx -y @runpod/mcp-server@latest`
- **sophia-agi** — `python sophia_mcp/server.py`

## AI assistants used during development

- **Claude (Anthropic)** _(proprietary)_ — code authoring, review, and documentation under human direction — AI assistants are tools; authorship and responsibility for all results remain with the human maintainer.
- **GitHub Copilot** _(proprietary)_ — code review and suggestions on pull requests
- **GLM** _(proprietary)_ — advisory review on pull requests

## Measurement / judge inference services

- **OpenRouter** _(proprietary)_ — hosted inference for LLM-judge families used in evaluation — Used only to grade aggregates; raw judge prompts in held-out sets are never published.
- **LLMHub** _(proprietary)_ — second independent inference provider for the >=2-judge-family rule

## Metered compute providers

- **RunPod** _(proprietary)_ — metered GPU jobs for training/eval, driven exclusively through GitHub Actions — The only metered compute path; owned hardware (DGX Spark / Mac Studio) is preferred where possible.

---

_Proprietary tools are marked; the project prefers open, non-proprietary alternatives and owned hardware where feasible (a Leiden Declaration recommendation). Known gaps in non-proprietary coverage are tracked in [LEIDEN-ALIGNMENT.md](LEIDEN-ALIGNMENT.md)._
